summary: A transition-and-counter 4-2-3-1 that may flirt with a back-5, attacking direct and vertical — but with lots of open space defensively.
style: 4-2-3-1 (could flirt with a back-5) — transition and counter-attacking, vertical play to attack direct.
strengths: Taremi's hold-up and link play to spring attacks

keyPlayers.name: Mehdi Taremi
keyPlayers.note: Goals, hold-up and link-up play, moments out of nothing — in-form for his nation

tactics: Defend deep, attack vertically and break in transition with Taremi leading the line. The defensive spaces are the obvious vulnerability.
sources: John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
